story.title: Dr. Huang's Energy Coaster
story.text:
Hello! I am Dr. Mei-Ling Huang. I am a Roller Coaster Dynamics Engineer. I design amazing rides that are also very safe. To do my job, I must be an expert on energy.

Think about the first big hill on a coaster. A strong chain pulls the cars all the way to the top. As the coaster gets higher, it stores more and more energy. We call this stored energy potential energy. It is waiting to be used.

Then, whoosh! The coaster rolls down the hill. All that stored potential energy transforms into the energy of motion. This is called kinetic energy. The more potential energy we start with, the more kinetic energy we get. That means the coaster goes faster at the bottom.

But why does the coaster eventually stop? The wheels are always rubbing on the steel track. This rubbing creates a force called friction. Friction changes some of the useful kinetic energy into heat. We call this heat loss. The energy is not destroyed, it just warms up the track and wheels. This is the law of energy conservation. I make sure there is always enough energy to finish the ride safely.
think_abouts: What happens to the stored energy when the coaster goes down the hill?
Why does friction make the coaster slow down?
story.go_deeper: If a coaster has to go through two big loops, would Dr. Huang need to make the first hill taller or shorter? Why?

margin_defs.definition: Stored energy from being high up.
A force that slows things down.

check_prompts: What is one thing Dr. Huang must think about to make a roller coaster safe?

vocabulary.definition: Stored energy an object has because of its position or height — like a roller coaster waiting at the top of a hill.
vocabulary.use_sentence: The coaster at the top of the hill has a lot of potential energy.

vocabulary.definition: The energy of motion — the faster something moves, the more kinetic energy it has.
vocabulary.use_sentence: As the coaster speeds down the hill, it gains kinetic energy.

vocabulary.definition: A force that happens when two surfaces rub together, slowing things down and creating heat.
vocabulary.use_sentence: Friction between the wheels and the track makes the ride slow down.

vocabulary.definition: Energy that transforms into heat because of friction — this energy leaves the motion system.
vocabulary.use_sentence: The coaster loses speed because of heat loss from friction.

vocabulary.definition: The scientific law that energy is never created or destroyed — it only changes form.
vocabulary.use_sentence: Energy conservation means the total energy in the system stays the same, even when it changes form.

discussion_cards.prompt: Think about a swing on a playground. Where does a swing have the most potential energy? Where does it have the most kinetic energy?
discussion_cards.frame: A swing has the most potential energy when ___ and the most kinetic energy when ___.

discussion_cards.prompt: Dr. Huang wants to make a new coaster go faster at the bottom of the first hill. What is one thing she could change about her design?
discussion_cards.frame: To make the coaster faster, Dr. Huang could change the ___ because ___.

discussion_cards.prompt: Besides the track, what else might cause friction or air resistance on a real roller coaster and slow it down?
discussion_cards.frame: Something else that could cause friction is ___.

reflection_frames.knew: Before reading, I already knew that ___
reflection_frames.learned: From the story, I learned that ___
